rationale.R1.1: A generic section review centrally includes checking whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are clear and coherent.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies verifying whether the architecture, ambiguity-handling mechanism, stability claim, and conclusions form a consistent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The review should assess whether the reported experiments and metrics support the robustness, naturalness, and deployment claims made in the section.
rationale.R1.5: Reviewing a section commonly includes evaluating paragraph ordering and the section-level narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: A section review centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty because the terse delegation does not specify a narrower review lens.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility must include avoiding endorsement of claims beyond what the section and evidence establish.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits or broader paper work.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns robot teleoperation and deployment, review responsibilities include sensitivity to downstream safety and misuse risks.
rationale.RX.5: Any review should remain traceable to the provided section rather than unstated external assumptions.
